channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: SEC just hit four companies with big fines for downplaying the SolarWinds hack
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission fined four companies on Tuesday with misleading investors about the impact the 2020 hack of SolarWinds had on their own systems.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: White House reviewing HIPAA cybersecurity updates
item.description: HHS wants to boost the cybersecurity protections required under HIPAA, as ransomware groups continue to target sensitive healthcare information. The post White House reviewing HIPAA cybersecurity updates first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: How Anthropic’s new ‘computer use’ ability could further AI automation
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude 3.5 Sonnet large language model has gained a new ability: operating a computer. The new ability, which the company is calling “computer use,” is currently in beta test.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: US banks brace for open banking era of consumer data sharing
item.description: The measure effectively breaks banks’ hold on crucial data from savings patterns and checking account data to rent payment history — a set up where some firms made switching harder to help boost profits.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: PETRILA, Romania (AP) — For many years, Sebastian Tirinticǎ worked in a coal mine, just like his father and grandfather before him.
item.dc:creator: ANCA GURZU, Cipher News, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: German Federal Foreign Office uses AI to get personal
item.description: With about 12,000 employees worldwide, along with offices in Bonn and Berlin and approximately 230 missions, the reach of the German Federal Foreign Office is vast, connecting with citizens abroad, along with other governments and international organizations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The power of prime numbers in computing
item.description: Everyone knows that a prime number is one that cannot be made by multiplying other whole numbers together. This deceptively simple attribute hides a world of complexity that has engaged mathematicians ancient and modern.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Commercial space companies now able to churn out satellites
item.description: Increased satellite production is now becoming the standard now in the space hardware industry. The post Commercial space companies now able to churn out satellites first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: ABBA, Radiohead and The Cure musicians sign AI protest letter against 'unlicensed use' of works
item.description: Musicians from ABBA, Radiohead and The Cure have joined actors and authors in signing a protest letter against the mining of their artistry to build artificial intelligence tools.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Flying air taxis move closer to US takeoff with issuing of FAA rule
item.description: Federal regulators are giving a strong push to electric-powered air taxis by issuing a final rule for operating the aircraft and how pilots will be trained to fly them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: cPacket doubles the speed of its packet capture appliance
item.description: cPacket Networks this week launched a new appliance designed to capture network traffic and enable network operations teams to achieve better efficiency by monitoring network reliability in real time while also looking backward to investigate prior incidents.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: FTC's rule banning fake online reviews goes into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal rule banning fake online reviews is now in effect. The Federal Trade Commission issued the rule in August banning the sale or purchase of online reviews.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: GoFundMe bets social media can unlock Gen Z giving. A Meta partnership and new tools will test that
item.description: New GoFundMe tools will make it easier to circulate causes across online platforms in a push to cater toward younger generations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fake IT workers from North Korea have started blackmailing their victims
item.description: A new report from Secureworks shows that the North Korean group Nickel Tapestry has expanded its operations from getting North Korean IT workers illegally employed by companies in other countries to allowing the workers to steal data that can be used for extortion if they’re fired, according to The
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Buyer’s guide: Edge data centers
item.description: Edge data centers include hardware, software, applications, data management, connectivity, gateways, security, and advanced analytics. They come in all shapes and sizes depending on the use case: Small ones can fit into a wiring closet. Larger ones resemble traditional data centers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: An electric aircraft the military has its eyes on can take off with only 150 feet of runway
item.description: Natural disasters like Hurricane Helene in North Carolina illustrate the need for aircraft capable of accessing remote or disaster-stricken areas with minimal infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stanford researchers map ‘White-Only’ properties using AI
item.description: Racially restrictive covenants are clauses in property deeds that ban individuals of a particular race from buying a home.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: DoD ‘fine tunes’ final CMMC program rule, industry turns attention to implementation
item.description: The Pentagon made some key tweaks to the final CMMC program rule. Several program watchers said the changes should help simplify a complex compliance program. The post DoD ‘fine tunes’ final CMMC program rule, industry turns attention to implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: 6 key mobile and IoT/OT attack trend findings
item.description: The role of the CISO continues to expand, driven by the rising number of breaches and cyberattacks like ransomware, as well as SEC requirements for public organizations to disclose material breaches.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: HPE, Dell launch another round of AI servers
item.description: The AI spin continues, with Hewlett Packard Enterprise and Dell Technologies both introducing new servers oriented towards training large language models (LLM).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Alabama man arrested for hacking Securities and Exchange Commission X account
item.description: Following the unauthorized announcement made during the hack, the DOJ said the price of bitcoin increased by more than $1,000 per bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Logan Sparkman, NewsNation

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Sextortion occurs when a victim is coerced to provide sexually explicit photos or videos of themselves and threatened with them being shared publicly.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Tlaib calls out Kroger for facial recognition practices
item.description: Kroger said it uses facial recognition to present targeted ads on its digital price tags that it rolled out five years ago.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Kernstine, NewsNation

item.title: Easy ways to make calls when vision is a challenge
item.description: For those with visual challenges, there are a number of voice-dialing apps and features to help make phone calls without having to see numbers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: iOS 18: Maximize your privacy by turning off these iPhone settings now
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals some of the iPhone settings you may want to turn off to improve privacy after updating to iOS 18.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
